old_claim.note: The Regenstrief system maintained a core computerized medical record and captured structured patient data; relational DBMS is the closest current graph node for this data-management capability.

new_claim.note: The Regenstrief system maintained a core computerized medical record and captured structured patient data, requiring database-management capability without implying a relational DBMS.

source_shape.source_locator: Regenstrief abstract describing a computer-based medical record system in operation since 1973.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The article describes a computer-based record system containing core medical-record data and supporting efficient use and maintenance of patient records.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports database-management capability for structured electronic records while not supporting a relational-DBMS claim for the 1973 system.

ontology_before: EHR depended on relational DBMS, over-specifying the data-management implementation for early computer-based medical records.
ontology_after: EHR depends on generic database-management systems, with relational DBMS remaining a later implementation family.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: electronic_health_records no longer directly depends on databases_relational_dbms and now depends on database_management_systems.
would_reject_if: A source showed the 1973 Regenstrief system required relational DBMS.
The graph reintroduced direct EHR to relational-DBMS dependency.
The EHR node were re-scoped specifically to modern relational EHR database products.
short_reason: Early EHR needs database management, not necessarily relational DBMS.
